title: Rich Roasted Argentinian Courgettes Frittata
description: A fresh and gooey Argentinian dish featuring roasted courgettes, perfect for a brunch mood, with a double fry technique inspired by KFC, and low sodium friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, parmesan cheese, salt, black pepper, and chili flakes.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the garlic until fragrant.
Add the sliced courgettes to the skillet and cook until they are tender and lightly browned.
In a separate pan, heat about 1/2 inch of olive oil over medium high heat and double fry the courgettes until crispy.
In a greased 9 inch oven safe skillet, arrange the roasted courgettes in a single layer.
Pour the egg mixture over the courgettes and cook over medium heat until the edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes or until the eggs are almost set.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top.
Return the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 2 minutes or until the eggs are fully set.
